en.title: What Kind of Friend Are You?
en.subtitle: The role you play when it matters most
en.description: Find out what kind of friend people are lucky to have in you
en.shareText: As a friend, I'm the one who...

ko.title: 나는 어떤 친구일까?
ko.subtitle: 중요한 순간에 내가 맡는 역할
ko.description: 사람들이 너를 친구로 둬서 다행인 이유를 알아보자

en.text:
Your friend just got dumped.
What do you do?

ko.text:
친구가 방금 차였다
뭘 할 거야?

en.text: I'm at their door with snacks before they even ask

ko.text: 연락 오기 전에 과자 들고 문 앞에 간다

options.rationale: Immediate mobilization (Ignite) + empathic instinct (Pulse) + friend-priority (Bond)

en.text: I listen for hours without trying to fix anything

ko.text: 고치려 하지 않고 몇 시간이든 그냥 들어준다

options.rationale: Patient presence (Observe) + emotional holding space (Pulse) + other-care (Bond)

en.text: I give them space first and check in when they're ready

ko.text: 일단 공간을 주고 준비되면 연락한다

options.rationale: Calculated timing (Observe) + strategic support assessment (Prism) + respectful care (Bond)

en.text: I tell them honestly what I think happened and why

ko.text: 왜 그렇게 된 건지 솔직하게 내 생각을 말해준다

options.rationale: Direct verbal action (Ignite) + analytical perspective (Prism) + honest self-expression priority (Self)

en.text:
Your friend is about to make
a decision you think is a mistake

ko.text:
친구가 실수할 것 같은
결정을 하려고 한다

en.text: I speak up — they need to hear this even if it's uncomfortable

ko.text: 말한다 — 불편해도 들어야 하니까

options.rationale: Confrontational honesty (Ignite) + logical assessment (Prism) + own-standards expression (Self)

en.text: I share my concerns gently and let them decide

ko.text: 걱정되는 부분 조심스럽게 말하고 결정은 맡긴다

options.rationale: Measured approach (Observe) + reasoned concern (Prism) + autonomy-respecting care (Bond)

en.text: I support whatever they choose — it's their life after all

ko.text: 뭘 선택하든 응원한다 — 결국 그 사람 인생이니까

options.rationale: Non-interventionist stance (Observe) + empathic acceptance (Pulse) + unconditional support (Bond)

en.text: I lay out the pros and cons so they can see clearly

ko.text: 장단점을 정리해서 명확하게 볼 수 있게 해준다

options.rationale: Active contribution (Ignite) + structured analysis (Prism) + clarity as self-standard (Self)

en.text:
You haven't heard from a close friend
in weeks. What do you do?

ko.text:
친한 친구한테 몇 주째
연락이 없다 어떻게 해?

en.text: I call them right now — silence this long means something's up

ko.text: 바로 전화한다 — 이 정도 침묵은 뭔가 있는 거

options.rationale: Immediate outreach (Ignite) + intuitive concern (Pulse) + friend-checking priority (Bond)

en.text: I send a low-pressure text — 'thinking of you, no need to reply'

ko.text: 부담 없는 문자를 보낸다 — '생각나서 답 안 해도 돼'

options.rationale: Gentle non-intrusive approach (Observe) + emotional warmth (Pulse) + other's space respect (Bond)

en.text: I figure they'll reach out when they're ready — I respect their space

ko.text: 준비되면 연락하겠지 — 공간을 존중한다

options.rationale: Independent non-attachment (Observe) + rational boundary-setting (Prism) + own-pace comfort (Self)

en.text: I ask mutual friends if they've heard anything first

ko.text: 공통 친구들한테 뭐 들은 거 없는지 먼저 물어본다

options.rationale: Active intelligence-gathering (Ignite) + strategic information collection (Prism) + concerned care (Bond)

en.text:
Pick the friend moment
that means the most to you

ko.text:
가장 소중한
친구와의 순간은?

en.text: Late night deep talks about life and everything

ko.text: 밤새 인생 얘기 하는 시간

options.rationale: Reflective shared space (Observe) + emotional depth exchange (Pulse) + intimate connection (Bond)

en.text: Spontaneous adventures nobody planned

ko.text: 아무도 계획 안 한 즉흥 모험

options.rationale: Spontaneous initiative (Ignite) + excitement-driven (Pulse) + freedom-in-friendship (Self)

en.text: Being there for each other during the hard stuff

ko.text: 힘든 시기에 서로 옆에 있어준 것

options.rationale: Steadfast presence (Observe) + emotional solidarity (Pulse) + mutual devotion (Bond)

en.text: Working on a project or goal together and nailing it

ko.text: 같이 프로젝트나 목표를 해내는 것

options.rationale: Collaborative action (Ignite) + strategic achievement (Prism) + performance satisfaction (Self)

en.text:
Two friends are fighting
and both come to you. You...

ko.text:
친구 둘이 싸웠는데
둘 다 너한테 온다 어떻게 해?

en.text: I tell each one what I honestly think — even if they don't like it

ko.text: 각각한테 솔직하게 내 생각을 말한다 — 싫어해도

options.rationale: Direct truth-telling (Ignite) + objective assessment (Prism) + integrity-first (Self)

en.text: I listen to both sides fully before saying anything

ko.text: 양쪽 다 끝까지 듣고 나서 말한다

options.rationale: Full-picture gathering (Observe) + balanced analysis (Prism) + fairness to both (Bond)

en.text: I try to get them in the same room to talk it out together

ko.text: 같은 방에 모아서 직접 대화하게 한다

options.rationale: Active mediation (Ignite) + emotional resolution instinct (Pulse) + relationship repair priority (Bond)

en.text: I stay out of it — they need to figure this out themselves

ko.text: 끼어들지 않는다 — 본인들이 해결해야 할 문제다

options.rationale: Non-involvement boundary (Observe) + rational distance (Prism) + own-peace protection (Self)

en.text:
Your friend group is planning a trip.
What's your role?

ko.text:
친구들이랑 여행을 간다
너의 역할은?

en.text: I make the itinerary — someone has to or we'll wander aimlessly

ko.text: 일정 짠다 — 누가 안 하면 그냥 떠돌게 되니까

options.rationale: Organizational initiative (Ignite) + structural planning (Prism) + group-serving role (Bond)

en.text: I'm the hype — I keep energy up and find unexpected fun

ko.text: 분위기 담당 — 에너지 올리고 예상 못한 재미를 찾는다

options.rationale: Energy injection (Ignite) + spontaneous fun-finding (Pulse) + self-expressive role (Self)

en.text: I make sure everyone's comfortable and nobody feels left out

ko.text: 다 편한지 확인하고 빠지는 사람 없게 챙긴다

options.rationale: Attentive monitoring (Observe) + emotional caretaking (Pulse) + inclusion priority (Bond)

en.text: I go with the flow — wherever the group goes I'm happy

ko.text: 다 따라간다 — 어디든 같이면 좋다

options.rationale: Passive flow-following (Observe) + contentment-based (Pulse) + low-demand self-comfort (Self)

en.text:
What do your friends probably
come to you for most?

ko.text:
친구들이 너한테
주로 뭘 찾아오는 것 같아?

en.text: A brutally honest opinion they can't get anywhere else

ko.text: 다른 데서 못 듣는 진짜 솔직한 의견

options.rationale: Verbal directness (Ignite) + analytical perspective (Prism) + unfiltered self-expression (Self)

en.text: A warm place to just exist without being judged

ko.text: 판단 없이 그냥 있을 수 있는 따뜻한 공간

options.rationale: Accepting presence (Observe) + emotional safety creation (Pulse) + unconditional acceptance (Bond)

en.text: Energy and fun when they need a break from everything

ko.text: 다 잊고 놀고 싶을 때 에너지와 재미

options.rationale: Active energy provision (Ignite) + fun-generating instinct (Pulse) + entertainer self-expression (Self)

en.text: A solid plan when they don't know what to do next

ko.text: 뭘 해야 할지 모를 때 확실한 방향

options.rationale: Directive guidance (Ignite) + strategic solution-building (Prism) + service to friend (Bond)

en.text:
What's the hardest part
of being a friend for you?

ko.text:
친구 하면서
가장 힘든 부분은?

en.text: Watching them make mistakes I already warned them about

ko.text: 이미 말해줬는데 같은 실수하는 거 지켜보는 것

options.rationale: Forced observation of predicted outcome (Observe) + analytical frustration (Prism) + own-standard imposition conflict (Self)

en.text: Not knowing if I'm doing enough for the people I love

ko.text: 내가 충분히 해주고 있는 건지 모르겠을 때

options.rationale: Self-evaluating uncertainty (Observe) + emotional care anxiety (Pulse) + relationship adequacy concern (Bond)

en.text: Keeping up with everyone when I'm running low myself

ko.text: 나도 힘든데 다 챙기는 게 버거울 때

options.rationale: Ongoing active care (Ignite) + emotional labor fatigue (Pulse) + over-commitment to others (Bond)

en.text: Losing touch with people because life just gets busy

ko.text: 바빠서 자연스럽게 멀어지는 것

options.rationale: Passive drift acceptance (Observe) + rational time-priority (Prism) + self-focused life management (Self)

en.title: I'm the First Call
en.description:
When life falls apart, your friends reach for you before anyone else. You don't wait for an invitation to show up — you just appear, usually with exactly what they need, whether that's a plan or just your presence. Being needed isn't a burden for you. It's where you feel most alive.

You're the one who drops everything when a friend calls at 2 AM. You reorganize your week around someone else's crisis without even thinking about it. When something good happens, you're the first to celebrate too — you probably send longer congratulations texts than most people's wedding speeches. In group settings, you're the connective tissue. You remember who's going through what, you check in unprompted, and you make people feel like they're not alone even when they haven't asked for help.

People trust you with the messy, unfiltered version of their lives because you've earned it — by showing up consistently, not just when it's convenient. You're the friend who makes other people feel lucky to have you, and they probably tell you that more than you realize.

But here's what you might not notice: you give so much that you sometimes forget to check whether anyone's giving back. You can end up in friendships where you're always the one reaching out, always the one adjusting, always the one carrying the emotional weight. That's not friendship — that's a one-way road. The people who deserve your energy will show up for you too. Let them prove it.

I don't wait to be invited. I just show up.

ko.title: 가장 먼저 전화하는 사람
ko.description:
인생이 무너질 때 친구들이 누구보다 먼저 너한테 손을 뻗어. 초대를 기다리지 않고 나타나는 사람이야 — 계획이든 밥이든 그냥 존재이든 딱 필요한 걸 들고 와. 필요한 사람이 되는 게 부담이 아니라 가장 나다운 순간이야.

새벽 2시에 친구가 전화하면 다 제쳐두고 받는 게 너야. 누군가의 위기에 맞춰서 일주일 일정을 바꿔도 고민도 안 해. 좋은 일이 생겨도 제일 먼저 축하하는 사람이지 — 축하 문자가 축사보다 긴 적 있을걸? 그룹에서 누가 뭘 겪고 있는지 기억하고 물어보지 않아도 챙겨주는 연결 조직이 너야.

사람들이 꾸미지 않은 지저분한 모습을 너한테 보여주는 건 그만큼 신뢰를 쌓아왔기 때문이야. 편할 때만이 아니라 꾸준히 나타나서 얻은 거야. 주변에서 "너 같은 친구 둔 게 행운"이라는 말 생각보다 많이 들을걸.

근데 너무 많이 주다 보면 돌아오는지 확인하는 걸 잊을 수 있어. 항상 네가 먼저 연락하고 항상 네가 맞추고 항상 네가 감정 무게를 지는 관계라면 그건 우정이 아니라 일방통행이야. 네 에너지를 받을 자격이 있는 사람은 너한테도 나타나줄 거야. 증명하게 해봐.

초대받기를 기다리지 않아. 그냥 나타나는 사람

en.title: I'm the Safe Space
en.description:
You don't fix, judge, or rush — you hold. When your friends come to you, they're not looking for answers. They're looking for a place where they can stop performing and just exist. And somehow, without saying much, you give them exactly that.

You're the friend people call when they need to cry without being told to stop. When someone's venting, you don't interrupt with advice — you let them finish, and then you say the one thing that makes them feel actually heard. You notice when someone's "fine" isn't fine. You pick up on shifts in energy that everyone else misses. People have probably told you things they've never told anyone else, and you carry those secrets with a kind of quiet reverence.

Your presence is genuinely healing. Not in a dramatic way — in the way that a quiet room and a warm drink can be healing. People leave conversations with you feeling lighter, even when nothing got solved. You create emotional safety so naturally that people don't even realize they've dropped their guard until they're already being honest.

Here's what to watch: being the safe space for everyone can quietly exhaust you. You absorb a lot of other people's pain, and you might not always process your own because you're so focused on theirs. And sometimes people mistake your patience for having no limits. You do have limits. Learning to hold space for yourself — not just for others — is how you stay whole.

I don't fix people. I let them feel safe enough to fix themselves.

ko.title: 안전한 공간이 되는 사람
ko.description:
고치지도 판단하지도 재촉하지도 않고 그냥 품어주는 사람이야. 친구들이 너한테 올 때 답을 찾는 게 아니라 연기하지 않고 그냥 있을 수 있는 자리를 찾는 거야. 넌 많은 말 없이도 딱 그걸 줘.

울어야 할 때 "그만 울어"라는 말 안 들을 수 있는 친구가 너야. 누가 쏟아내고 있으면 조언으로 끊지 않고 다 들어주고 나서 진짜 들렸다고 느끼게 하는 한 마디를 해. "괜찮아"가 괜찮지 않다는 걸 알아채고 다른 사람이 놓치는 에너지 변화를 잡아내지. 아무한테도 안 한 이야기를 너한테 한 적 있는 사람이 꽤 있을 거야.

네 존재 자체가 치유야. 거창한 게 아니라 조용한 방과 따뜻한 차 같은 그런 거. 아무것도 해결 안 됐는데 너랑 대화하고 나면 가벼워져. 감정적 안전을 너무 자연스럽게 만들어서 상대방이 솔직해지고 나서야 경계를 내렸다는 걸 알아챌 정도야.

근데 모두의 안전한 공간이 되다 보면 조용히 지쳐. 남의 아픔을 많이 흡수하면서 정작 네 감정은 처리 못 할 때가 있어. 네 인내심을 한계가 없다고 착각하는 사람도 있는데 한계는 있거든. 남한테뿐 아니라 너 자신한테도 공간을 만들어야 온전히 남아 있을 수 있어.

고치는 게 아니라 스스로 고칠 수 있을 만큼 안전하게 해주는 사람

en.title: I Tell You the Truth
en.description:
Your friends don't come to you for comfort — they come for clarity. You're the one who says what everyone's thinking but nobody has the guts to say out loud. Not to be cruel. Because you genuinely believe the truth is more useful than a comfortable lie.

You're the friend who says "honestly? that was your fault" when everyone else is nodding along. When someone asks for your opinion, they already know they're going to get it unfiltered — and that's exactly why they ask. You don't sugarcoat, but you're not reckless either. Your honesty comes from caring enough to risk the discomfort. People might flinch at what you say, but they almost always admit you were right.

The people who stick around you are the ones who value growth over comfort. Your friendships might be smaller in number but they're unusually honest — you and your close friends can say things to each other that most people never would. That kind of radical honesty creates a rare depth of trust.

Here's the edge you walk though: timing matters as much as truth. Being right doesn't help if the person isn't ready to hear it. You might occasionally mistake bluntness for helpfulness — sometimes people need a beat of empathy before they can absorb your clarity. Reading when someone needs the truth versus when they need to be held first is your next-level skill. The honesty is there. The precision of when to deploy it is the upgrade.

I don't tell you what you want to hear. I tell you what you need to hear.

ko.title: 진짜를 말해준다
ko.description:
친구들이 너한테 위로를 구하러 오는 게 아니라 명확함을 구하러 와. 모두가 생각하지만 아무도 말 안 하는 걸 너는 말하는 사람이야. 잔인해서가 아니라 진실이 편한 거짓말보다 유용하다고 진짜로 믿으니까.

다들 고개만 끄덕일 때 "솔직히? 그건 네 잘못이었어"라고 말하는 친구가 너야. 의견을 물으면 필터 없이 나온다는 걸 이미 아는데 그래서 묻는 거야. 포장은 안 하지만 무책임하지도 않아. 불편함을 감수할 만큼 신경 쓰니까 솔직한 거야. 그 순간엔 움찔해도 나중에 맞았다고 인정하는 경우가 거의 대부분이야.

네 곁에 남는 사람은 편안함보다 성장을 택하는 사람들이야. 숫자는 적어도 비정상적으로 솔직한 우정이지 — 대부분이 절대 안 할 말을 서로 할 수 있는 관계야. 그런 급진적 솔직함이 흔치 않은 깊이의 신뢰를 만들어.

근데 타이밍이 진실만큼 중요해. 맞는 말이어도 상대가 들을 준비가 안 됐으면 소용없거든. 가끔 직접적인 게 도움이라고 착각할 수 있는데 때로는 공감이 먼저 필요해. 진실이 필요한 순간과 안아주는 게 먼저인 순간을 읽는 게 다음 레벨이야. 솔직함은 이미 있으니까 언제 꺼내는지가 업그레이드야.

듣고 싶은 말이 아니라 들어야 할 말을 해주는 사람

en.title: I've Got the Plan
en.description:
When your friend doesn't know what to do, you do. You're not just emotional support — you're strategic support. Where others see a wall, you see a door, a window, and two backup exits you mapped out while they were still panicking.

You're the one who turns a friend's crisis into an action plan within twenty minutes. Breakup? You've got a timeline for healing, a list of distractions, and you've already blocked the ex on their behalf. Job search? You've rewritten their resume before they even asked. You show love through logistics — planning the trip, organizing the birthday, making sure nobody forgot to book the restaurant. People joke that you're the "group mom" or the "CEO of friendship," and honestly, you probably are.

Your friends rely on you because you make the impossible feel manageable. You break big scary things into small doable steps, and that ability to create order from chaos is genuinely rare. When everyone's overwhelmed, you're the calm in the storm — clipboard in hand, figuratively or literally.

But here's the thing: not everything needs a plan. Sometimes a friend just wants to vent, and you're already building a spreadsheet. Your instinct to fix can make people feel like their feelings are a problem to solve rather than an experience to share. Learning to ask "do you want me to help fix this, or just listen?" before jumping into action mode is how your planning superpower becomes even more powerful.

I don't just support you. I build you a roadmap.

ko.title: 계획이 있는 사람
ko.description:
친구가 뭘 해야 할지 모를 때 너는 알아. 감정적 지지만이 아니라 전략적 지지를 하는 사람이야. 남들이 벽만 보는 데서 너는 문이랑 창문이랑 비상구 두 개를 이미 파악하고 있거든.

친구의 위기를 20분 안에 액션 플랜으로 바꾸는 게 너야. 이별? 회복 타임라인에 기분전환 리스트에 전 연인 차단까지 대신 해놨어. 취업? 부탁하기도 전에 이력서를 고쳐놨을 걸. 여행 짜고 생일 준비하고 레스토랑 예약 까먹은 거 없는지 확인하는 사람 — 사랑을 물류로 표현하는 타입이야. "그룹 엄마" "우정의 CEO"라는 농담 들어봤을 텐데 솔직히 맞아.

불가능해 보이는 걸 감당 가능하게 만들어주니까 친구들이 기대. 크고 무서운 걸 작고 할 수 있는 단계로 쪼개는 능력이 진짜 희귀해. 다들 압도당할 때 폭풍 속의 고요함이 너야.

근데 모든 게 계획이 필요한 건 아니야. 친구가 그냥 쏟아내고 싶은데 넌 이미 스프레드시트를 만들고 있을 수 있어. 고치려는 본능이 상대 감정을 공유할 경험이 아니라 해결할 문제로 느끼게 만들 수 있거든. "이거 도와줄까 아니면 그냥 들어줄까?" 먼저 물어보는 법을 배우면 네 계획 초능력이 더 강력해져.

응원만 하는 게 아니라 로드맵을 만들어주는 사람
